threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'bargain' means anything under £10 at the market. Locals know it’s all about how much you can haggle.
Caught the N68 last week, and a guy brought a full kettle onboard. He claimed it was for 'brewing up' while we waited at red lights. Truly a Brixton moment.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids setting up a street art mural while a cat watched from a windowsill—only in Neukölln do artistic chaos and feline judgment coexist so perfectly.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a 'silent disco' in an abandoned factory, where DJs spin while everyone wears headphones. It's surreal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local doesn't flinch at the sounds of street repairs at 1 AM but will grumble if the burrito place runs out of guac.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog casually sunbathing on the tracks at Civic Center yesterday. Muni couldn’t care less, just sat there while we all waited.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. They miss our local fiestas, like Festa Major, where the real heart of the neighborhood beats.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still look bewildered at the 3pm siesta silence, while clutching their guidebook like it's a treasure map.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still expect the Tube to run on time. Locals wear that knowing smile when the announcements go off. You’ll learn soon enough.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Selah Sue. Even the driver was chuckling while stopping at every traffic light.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I watched a guy in a full suit order a burger at 3 AM from the Kebab stand on Sonnenallee, no shame.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do they have these crazy Kiez-Discos that pop up in abandoned buildings. The vibe? Pure underground magic with locals spinning vinyl.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the exact taco truck that’s still open at 2 AM after a long night.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy in a full tuxedo trying to chase down a bus while holding a bouquet of flowers. Classic SF chaos!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every street performer is talented. In reality, the saxophonist at Pl. del Sol has been playing the same tune for months now.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their untied sandals in January—classic tourist mistake. Locals know it gets chilly in the evenings.
